text: **Speaker 1:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**Speaker 1:** Jill.
**Speaker 1:** Welcome to Let's Learn English with Anna.
**Speaker 1:** We all {wear|着る/身につける|/wɛr/|I wear a jacket today.} clothes, but we do not wear the same kind of clothes.
**Speaker 1:** We can wear {bright|明るい/鮮やか|/braɪt/|Bright colors make me happy.} colors.
**Speaker 1:** Red, yellow, purple.
**Speaker 1:** We can wear black and white.
**Speaker 1:** We can wear {dressy|フォーマルな/派手な|/ˈdresi/|Dressy clothes for a wedding.} clothes or {sporty|スポーティーな|/ˈspɔːrti/|Sporty clothes for exercise.} clothes.
**Speaker 1:** We also wear different clothes for different {places|場所|/ˈpleɪsɪz/|Different places have different rules.} and events.
**Speaker 1:** Like school or a wedding.
**Speaker 1:** What are you wearing today?
**Speaker 1:** That is our question for today.
**Speaker 1:** Now let's visit Anna.
**Speaker 1:** She answers the question.
**Speaker 1:** What are you wearing today?
**Speaker 2:** Hi friends!
**Speaker 2:** Tonight I am going to a {party|パーティー|/ˈpɑːrti/|We have a party tonight.}
**Speaker 2:** So I am wearing my party clothes.
**Speaker 3:** You are talking about clothes.
**Speaker 3:** And our question for today is, what are you wearing today?
**Speaker 2:** What am I wearing today?
**Speaker 2:** This white coat is my party coat!
**Speaker 2:** These red shoes are my party shoes.
**Speaker 2:** Max, Daisy
**Speaker 2:** You are {invited|招待された|/ɪnˈvaɪtɪd/|You are invited to my birthday.} to the party too!
**Speaker 2:** I am invited, but I have nothing to wear.
**Speaker 3:** That is right, easy.
**Speaker 3:** You are a flower.
**Speaker 3:** Flowers do not wear clothes.
**Speaker 2:** I want party clothes.
**Speaker 2:** Do not {worry|心配しない}.
**Speaker 2:** I can help.
**Speaker 1:** This {shirt|シャツ} is my favorite.
**Speaker 1:** What is your favorite {clothing|衣服/服}?
**Speaker 1:** Next, Anna {teaches|教える} us words to help us talk about clothes.
**Speaker 2:** Let's talk about two words, this and these.
**Speaker 2:** We use this to talk about one thing.
**Speaker 2:** This shirt is {yellow|黄色}.
**Speaker 2:** We use these to talk about two or more things.
**Speaker 2:** These shirts are {orange|オレンジ色}.
**Speaker 2:** What changes in these two sentences?
**Speaker 2:** This shirt is yellow.
**Speaker 2:** These shirts are orange.
**Speaker 2:** This changed to these.
**Speaker 2:** Is changed to R.
**Speaker 2:** Shirt changed to shirts.
**Speaker 3:** And yellow changed to orange.
**Speaker 1:** Yes, Max.
**Speaker 1:** Thank you.
**Speaker 1:** Now let's {practice|練習する} the names of other types of clothes.
**Speaker 4:** This is a {skirt|スカート}.
**Speaker 4:** These are red skirts.
**Speaker 4:** These are blue {socks|靴下}.
**Speaker 4:** This is a pair of {shorts|半ズボン}.
**Speaker 4:** Here are two pairs of {pants|ズボン}.
**Speaker 4:** This is a black {coat|コート}.
**Speaker 1:** Great job!
**Speaker 1:** For {exercise|運動/エクササイズ}, I wear sporty clothes.
**Speaker 1:** For work, I wear work clothes.
**Speaker 1:** Now let's hear from some friends.
**Speaker 1:** They tell us what they {like|好む/好き|/laɪk/|I like pizza.} to {wear|着る/身につける|/wɛr/|I wear a blue shirt.}.
**Speaker 5:** This is my {favorite|お気に入りの|/ˈfeɪvərɪt/|This is my favorite book.} t-shirt.
**Speaker 5:** My t-shirt is {dirty|汚れた|/ˈdɜːrti/|The floor is dirty.}, so I need to {change|変える/交換する|/tʃeɪndʒ/|Can I change my shoes?} it.
**Speaker 5:** Now it is clean.
**Speaker 6:** This is my {favorite|お気に入りの|/ˈfeɪvərɪt/|This is my favorite toy.} dress.
**Speaker 6:** This is my {favorite|お気に入りの|/ˈfeɪvərɪt/|This is my favorite color.} skirt
**Speaker 1:** We can {buy|買う|/baɪ/|I buy a book.} clothes, but we can also {make|作る|/meɪk/|I make a cake.} clothes.
**Speaker 1:** So now let's go with Anna and {meet|会う/出会う|/miːt/|Let's meet at the park.} people who make clothes.
**Speaker 2:** This is my friend Ruby.
**Speaker 2:** She {makes|作る|/meɪks/|She makes cookies.} clothes.
**Speaker 2:** She {made|作った|/meɪd/|I made a cake yesterday.} the {scarf|マフラー|/skɑːrf/|She wears a warm scarf.}.
**Speaker 2:** And she {made|作った|/meɪd/|He made a painting.} this art.
**Speaker 7:** I {made|作った|/meɪd/|I made lunch.} this dress.
**Speaker 7:** I {made|作った|/meɪd/|She made a mistake.} this skirt.
**Speaker 7:** I {made|作った|/meɪd/|I made a cake.} these hats.
**Speaker 2:** I am ready for the {party|パーティー|/ˈpɑːrti/|We have a party tonight.}.
**Speaker 2:** Max, Daisy, you both look {great|素晴らしい|/greɪt/|That's a great idea.}
**Speaker 3:** I feel {great|素晴らしい|/greɪt/|I feel great today.}.
**Speaker 2:** I feel {amazing|驚くべき/素晴らしい|/əˈmeɪzɪŋ/|The view is amazing.}.
**Speaker 2:** My friend {made|作った|/meɪd/|I made dinner.} it for you, Daisy.
**Speaker 2:** Come on.
**Speaker 2:** Let's go to the {party|パーティー|/ˈpɑːrti/|We are going to a party.}!
**Speaker 1:** Now you can talk about your clothes.
**Speaker 1:** Use the words you learn here.
**Speaker 1:** And join us again next time on Let's Learn English with Anna.
